threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newcomers by their bewilderment at how many vegan cafes there are per square mile. It's like they're on a scavenger hunt.
Caught the 133 last night and the driver actually sang the route. Never seen that before. Kept us all entertained until Clapham!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid riding a bike in the middle of the street, dodging construction cones, while a dog chased after a pigeon. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see entire parks turning into open-air nightclubs, complete with DJ booths and makeshift bars. It's a weekend ritual.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their indignation over the bread selection at Tartine; tourists rave, but locals know the real gems are just a block away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver helping a lost tourist find their way to Dolores Park. Moments like these remind you that even in chaos, there's community.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think a siesta is just lying down for a nap. In Gràcia, it’s more about catching up with friends at the local bodega while the world slows down.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still look at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, squinting at each line with a puzzled frown.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newcomers—they're the ones still looking puzzled at the bus stops despite the 'Next Bus' screen showing delays.
Last Saturday, the 35 bus was packed with a group singing old Skints songs—plenty of shushing from the driver but no one cared.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found an old lady selling homemade jam on the corner of my street, while a group of kids played Fußball nearby. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a vegan döner after a night out, while the street art around you keeps changing every week.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name at least three taco trucks and remember the one that went missing in '17.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy reading a newspaper on the bus this morning, completely unfazed by the chaos of street performers and tourists outside. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about bars and restaurants, but many locals still have a thriving community life centered around small plazas and neighborhood traditions.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they always stand on the wrong side of the escalator, blocking the locals in a hurry.
